strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience with the core stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker/Kubernetes)
Current hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — a rare, explicitly required skill
Deep testing and craftsmanship culture: TDD, trunk-based development, authored test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught testing courses
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience, and no mention of Vitest/Jest/Playwright specifically despite strong general testing background
Kotlin absent and Java experience appears dated (robotics work 2016-2018), weakening the secondary backend stack fit
No explicit evidence of authentication/authorization or security best-practices work

key_factors.factor: Primary stack proficiency (TS/Node/React) with cloud and containers
Missing Nest.js and named testing frameworks (Vitest/Playwright)

response.justification: The role requires end-to-end fullstack delivery in TypeScript/Node/React, testing framework expertise, cloud/container knowledge, polyglot ability, and AI agent development. The candidate shows concrete evidence on nearly all: TypeScript/React/MongoDB/AWS CDK at Optilyz, Node/TypeScript/GraphQL with TDD and CI/CD at RIDE, Kubernetes deployment for GISCollective, and current MCP/agentic tooling work at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI agent requirement. Polyglot breadth (TS, DLang, C++, Java, Swift, PHP) is demonstrated, though Kotlin is absent and Java is dated. Testing culture is exceptionally strong (authored test libraries, taught TDD/Selenium courses), but the specific frameworks Vitest/Jest/Playwright and Nest.js are not evidenced, and security/auth work is not explicitly cited. A CS degree plus 6+ years is comfortably met. Gaps are narrow and learnable given the demonstrated adjacent depth.
